name: Val de Zafán Greenway Trail
name_original: Vía Verde del Val de Zafán entre Xerta y Fontcalda
description: Mountain bike the moderate 22.4-mile Vía Verde del Val de Zafán, exploring historic viaducts and the Fontcalda sanctuary.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Xerta, situated by the Ebro River in Catalonia, Spain, features diverse terrain for cyclists. The landscape is characterized by accessible greenways built on old railway lines, such as the Via Verda del Baix Ebre, and rugged mountain trails within Els Ports Natural Park. Riders can expect a mix of smooth, car-free paths, unpaved sections, and challenging climbs through olive groves and natural park environments. This area provides a variety of routes, from riverside paths to mountainous regions with significant elevation changes. Best mountain bike trails around Xerta The most popular mountain bike trail is Val de Zafán Greenway Trail, a 22.4 miles (36.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a converted railway line, featuring tunnels and viaducts. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierras de Cardó-el Boix – circular desde Tivenys, a difficult 19.6 miles (31.6 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain through the mountainous terrain of the Cardó-el Boix mountain range. Local mountain bikers also love the View of the Ebro River – Xerta Pier loop from Xerta, a 33.4 miles (53.7 km) trail leading through riverside landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Xerta is defined by greenways along the Ebro River, the rugged mountains of Els Ports Natural Park, and extensive olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy cycle touring to technically demanding mountain trails. The routes in Xerta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Xerta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Xerta?
A: There are over 90 mountain bike trails around Xerta, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include easy routes along converted railway lines and more challenging trails within the mountainous terrain of Els Ports Natural Park.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Xerta?
A: Yes, Xerta offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners and families. The region is known for its greenways, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945510912/val-de-zafan-greenway-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Val de Zafán Greenway Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which utilize old railway lines. These routes are predominantly flat, car-free, and feature smooth or compacted gravel surfaces, making them ideal for leisurely rides. There are 10 easy routes available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Xerta?
A: The terrain around Xerta is quite varied. You'll find smooth, car-free paths on the greenways, unpaved sections through olive groves, and rugged mountain trails with significant elevation changes within Els Ports Natural Park. This mix caters to different skill levels, from cycle touring to technically demanding mountain biking.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Xerta?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, the trails within Els Ports Natural Park offer physically and technically demanding routes. These can include steep, long inclines, difficult obstacles, and narrow, potentially slippery courses. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945405355/sierras-de-cardo-el-boix-circular-desde-tivenys" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Cardó-el Boix – circular desde Tivenys&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain. There are 31 difficult routes in the area.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Xerta?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Xerta are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10420744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ebro River – Xerta Pier loop from Xerta&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic circular ride through riverside landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38238717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de la Terra Alta – Xerta Pier loop from Xerta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Xerta offer access to several natural and historical attractions. You can ride across the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old railway viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the natural monument of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1044274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontcalda&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also provide views of the Ebro River and pass through traditional villages and extensive olive groves.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Xerta?
A: Xerta generally enjoys a Mediterranean climate, making spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking. The temperatures are milder, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months. Winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, though some higher mountain trails might be affected by weather.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Xerta?
A: The mountain biking experience in Xerta is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from the accessible greenways along the Ebro River to the rugged mountains of Els Ports Natural Park, and the scenic beauty of the olive groves and natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, particularly within the Els Ports Natural Park, you'll find routes with substantial elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14892698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Joan Baptista loop from Tivenys&lt;/a&gt; involves nearly 1,000 meters of ascent, making it a challenging option for those looking for a climb.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails in Xerta using public transport?
A: While Xerta itself is a smaller town, the greenways (Via Verda del Baix Ebre) are designed for cycle tourism and often connect to towns with public transport links. It's advisable to check local bus or train schedules to nearby larger towns like Tortosa, which may offer better connections, and then cycle to the trailheads.

Q: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the mountain bike trails?
A: Many routes pass through or near traditional Spanish villages where you can find local cafes and restaurants. The greenways, in particular, often utilize old railway stations that have been repurposed, with some even converted into restaurants or accommodations, such as in Benifallet, offering convenient spots for refreshments.
tips: This is an incredible location to admire the Ebro River as it passes through the Baix Ebre region.

Old train station in the municipality of Benifallet converted into tourist accommodation and a restaurant. The station is located in the middle of the Zafán Valley Greenway that joins Bajo Aragón and Baix Ebre.
At a lofty height, an old railway viaduct leads right through the gorge - and on the other side the Vía Verde disappears into a tunnel again.

Sanctuary located next to the Terra Alta Greenway within the area of the Pàndols-Cavalls mountains. The building, built in the 19th century, has a group of baroque-style buildings around it. The name of the sanctuary comes from the mineral-medicinal water source located to the left of the Canaleta river.

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán entre Xerta y Fontcalda?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 36 km (22 miles) long. For mountain biking, it typically takes around 2 hours and 37 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this Vía Verde?
faqs.answer: The terrain is largely flat and gently graded, as it follows an old railway line. You'll encounter compacted earth, paved sections, and hard gravel paths. Some parts might be unpaved dirt tracks. A notable feature is the presence of numerous tunnels and historic viaducts. Remember to bring lights for the tunnels!

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this Vía Verde is generally considered an easy trail, suitable for cyclists of all skill levels, including beginners and families. Its gentle gradient means there are no significant climbs, making for a comfortable ride.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, including views of rolling hills, lush valleys, dense forests, and dramatic cliffs. You'll pass olive and almond groves, vineyards, and open agricultural fields. Key highlights include the View of the Ebro River in Baix Ebre, the Benifallet Station, an Old railway viaduct, and the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fontcalda.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán entre Xerta y Fontcalda?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking near the starting point in Xerta, often close to the old railway station which serves as a trailhead. Towns along the route generally offer facilities and services, including parking options.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán entre Xerta y Fontcalda dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, greenways like this are generally dog-friendly. Please ensure your dog is kept on a leash and that you clean up after them to maintain the trail for everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal for mountain biking this Vía Verde. The weather is typically pleasant, and the landscapes are vibrant with blooming flora or rich autumn colors. Summers can be hot, so if riding then, start early to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use this Vía Verde?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy the Vía Verde del Val de Zafán entre Xerta y Fontcalda. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety tips for riding through the tunnels?
faqs.answer: Yes, many tunnels along the route are unlit. It's highly recommended to bring a headlamp or bike light to ensure visibility and safety when passing through them. Some tunnels can be quite long.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the TransCatalunya. De Ponent, Camí de Sant Jaume, Camí Natural del Baix Ebre, and Via Verda del Baix Ebre, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any places to stop for refreshments or a picnic?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fontcalda is a significant highlight near this section, offering a natural spa and a perfect spot for a refreshing stop and a picnic. You'll also find facilities and services in towns along the route.

waypoints.name: View of the Ebro River in Baix Ebre
Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fontcalda
